images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of CM-EVS. An expanded illustration of theCOVERpipeline (warping oracle, score, and point-cloud update) is in AppendixB.1(Figure9).
Figure 2:COVER’s three-phase per-scene pipeline (Algorithm1). Each iteration emits one ERP RGB-depth-pose frame plus its per-step provenance log.
Figure 3:CM-EVS: (a) multi-view4​π4\picoverage, (b) RGB + metric range depth + pose under one schema, (c) scene-type diversity across 13 unified buckets, and (d) low redundancy at scale.
Figure 4:Representative ERP frames per source. Each frame ships three modalities: RGB, range depth (Turbo colormap), and per-pixel Plücker ray direction(levoy1996lightfield,).
Figure 5:Room-type composition across CM-EVS and five baselines (13-bucket taxonomy). The weighted CM-EVS row (bottom) covers all 13 buckets.
Figure 6:Cross-source curator behavior (λ=0.35\lambda\!=\!0.35,τ=1%\tau\!=\!1\%early stop).(a)Per-step coverage gainGtG_{t}.(b)Per-scene frame-count distribution per source.

Figure 7:Selection diversity vs.λ\lambdaon a Blender multi-scene pool (K=30K\!=\!30): (a) unique scene prefixes hit, (b) per-prefix allocation, (c) pairwise Jaccard
similarity, (d) diversity vs. coverage.

Figure 8:Selection geometry on Blender (K=30K=30).λ=0\lambda=0collapses to a tight off-centre cluster inside the candidate pool;λ=0.2\lambda=0.2partially spreads;COVER’s defaultλ=0.35\lambda=0.35covers the pool.

Figure 9:Expanded overview ofCOVER: concretisation of the conflict-aware
warping oracle and the per-step state update, complementing Figure1in the main body.

Figure 10:Full selection geometry across three curator-source examples atK=30K\!=\!30. All feasible candidates (light) andCOVER-selected viewpoints (highlighted) are shown over the scene’s accumulated point cloud; the selected set spreads across the pool rather than clustering in any single region.

Figure 11:Per-step marginal coverage gainGtG_{t}across selection steps. The curves saturate at scene-specific rates; the production thresholdτ=1%\tau\!=\!1\%(dashed) defines the gain-gradient early stop.
Figure 12:Per-source range-depth distribution (violin) on released frames. Width reflects density at each depth; medians and 5–95% ranges are overlaid.
Figure 13:SixCOVER-selected viewpoints on a Blender indoor residential scene, spanning three functional zones; positions overlaid on the scene’s accumulated point cloud (right).
Figure 14:Low-redundancy selection on an open-plan office.Top:K=8K\!=\!8ERP views and footprint.Bottom:K=30K\!=\!30footprint withτ=1%\tau\!=\!1\%early stop.

Figure 15:Audited 50-bad-case gallery (Blender top, HM3D middle, ScanNet++ bottom). Each cell
shows the ERP RGB above the range depth at native 2:1; cell border colour and F1–F5 tag
follow Table8. Every cell shown isexcludedfrom the public
release.
